question: For the online platform, which interpretation best reflects what can (and cannot) be inferred from the 100% stacked bar chart?

options.A: Q1 has the highest number of online searches compared to the other quarters.
options.B: The chart allows us to compare the proportions of each metric within a quarter, but not their absolute counts across quarters.
options.C: The sum of all three metrics in each quarter shows how overall platform activity changes through the year.
options.D: All three metrics must have the same absolute value in every quarter because each bar reaches 100%.

explanation: 100% stacking normalizes unrelated categories to 100% within each quarter. Valid inferences include within-quarter ranking (e.g., one metric > another in every quarter). Invalid inferences include cross-quarter total comparisons or combining unrelated categories.
